evidence: Each frame carries one focus: the amber probe arrow with score in frame 1, the forming lilac dial in frame 2, and the filled meter plus aha sentence in frames 4-6.
At most the dial, three labels, and one sentence appear together, well under the four-novel-element ceiling.
The words oppose, ignore, and agree sit directly on the dial's arc positions rather than in a legend.
'The dot product is a similarity meter.' annotates the visible lilac meter directly beneath it, functioning as a key-term caption.
The dial labels, the '10.00' readout, and the aha sentence are all comfortably above the legibility threshold.
The dial, labels, sentence, and underline all sit fully inside the frame with generous margins.
No text elements overlap; the aha sentence and underline sit clear of the dial and labels.
Lilac carries the parameter/meter dial consistently, mint carries the converged aha sentence and its glowing underline, and amber marks the probe vector — all token-consistent.
Every element — dial, needle, labels, arrow, readout, sentence — carries propositional content with no decoration.

The lilac meter dial with oppose/ignore/agree, a swinging needle, and the aha sentence truing up with a mint glow are all present, though the promised 'a · b' mono readout annotation and the three explicit probe stills are absent.
The aha sentence and its underline sit directly below the meter they describe, keeping claim and referent together.
The sequence builds continuously: probe arrow and score, dial forming, labels appearing, dial filling, then sentence and mint underline truing up.
The composition is centered with ample negative space and a clear top-to-bottom reading order, aside from a faint stray glyph near the readout in frame 3.
